behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is not just local logic; classifier output must drive hold/publish state. LLM-DEP applies because correctness depends on real vision-model judgment of explicitness; stubbed outputs can hide failure. Rejection-gate: Workflow rejected because behavior is a single model decision plus routing, not multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Submit real explicit-image samples through the moderation service using the real vision model, then verify each submission is marked held-for-review and absent from published outputs.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP is primary: robustness to obfuscation is model-output quality, and deterministic mocks would always miss that risk. MOCK-HIDE: fake classifier responses would create false confidence. Rejection-gate: Workflow rejected because this behavior targets one model classification step, not a multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run a corpus of obfuscated hate-speech text variants through the real text model path and assert they are consistently flagged/held.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is primarily orchestration/state handoff across modules (image check result + text check result + decision/publish path). Integration is enough to catch drop/hold/publish wiring bugs. Integration-brake: (a) no explicit real OS/network/subprocess requirement in behavior text; (b) correctness here is publish routing after both checks pass, not model-quality evaluation; (c) no multi-agentic sequence requirement. Rejection-gate: System/Agentic rejected because behavior text does not require real external contract validation or classifier-quality assertions.
behaviors.test_description: With deterministic pass outcomes from both moderation components, execute the end-to-end in-process pipeline and verify the post is persisted/published and not present in held queues.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: MULTI-STEP applies directly: behavior explicitly requires the full sequence (image model step -> text model step -> publish decision). REAL-INFRA also matters for wall-clock SLA validation. Rejection-gate: System considered but rejected because text explicitly requires the full multi-step agentic pipeline; single-step system checks can miss sequencing effects across model stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end submissions through the real moderation pipeline with both real AI checks enabled, measure submit-to-final-decision latency, and assert p95 (or defined SLA metric) is <= 5 seconds under peak-like load.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: SYSTEM TRIAD #2 applies: this is an external error-path contract ('vision model is unavailable'). MOCK-HIDE: simple mocked failures can miss real timeout/connection/error semantics that determine retry vs fallback behavior. Rejection-gate: Agentic rejected because behavior is outage handling, not model-output quality; Workflow rejected because no multi-agentic chain is required.
behaviors.test_description: Induce real vision-model unavailability (e.g., endpoint down/timeout) in a system test, submit image posts, and verify they enter retry queue and are not auto-approved/published.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP is explicit: consistency for borderline content depends on real model behavior across repeated calls; stubs cannot reveal nondeterminism/drift. MOCK-HIDE: mocked deterministic outputs would always pass. Rejection-gate: Workflow considered but rejected because this is repeated evaluation of one model decision, not a multi-stage agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline-image sample repeatedly through the real vision moderation path within the target window and assert decision consistency meets the defined threshold.
